UKPI client segmentation and offer analytics

Synthetic-data portfolio dashboard. The page moves from the business question to the evidence, uncertainty, operational interpretation, and limits.

Evidence-led v2.2
Answer first

One communication plan is not enough for the simulated investor book.

The workflow separates 2,500 synthetic customers into 5 interpretable segments. The strongest simulated treatment-control result is 25.1 pp for CASH TO INVEST EDUCATION in Cash-heavy cautious investors.

Its approximate 95% interval is 13.3 pp to 36.9 pp. This is a synthetic experiment result, not evidence of real customer impact.

Synthetic customers
2,500
One customer-level feature mart
Segments retained
5
Interpretability trade-off
Mean stability ARI
0.992
Minimum 0.971 across five seeds
Quality checks needing review
1
Out of 11 automated checks
Decision path
1. Who is in the book?Size and profile the customer segments.
2. Why five groups?Compare cluster separation and multi-seed stability.
3. What appears to work?Estimate treatment-control uplift by segment and route.
4. How certain is it?Show sample sizes, uncertainty intervals, quality checks, and limits.
Model choice

Why retain five segments?

Honest interpretation

The highest silhouette score is at k=2 (0.231), while the retained five-cluster solution has a lower score of 0.147.

Therefore k=5 is not presented as mathematically optimal. It is retained as a business-facing trade-off: more useful service groups than k=2, while remaining highly stable across alternative random seeds.

Mean adjusted Rand index: 0.992; minimum: 0.971.

k Silhouette Inertia Decision

1

How many customers are in each segment?

Read this first

  • Segment size sets the operational scale.
  • High uplift in a small group may have less total impact than moderate uplift in a large group.
  • The table gives both count and share.
Segment Customers Share
Emerging monthly investors 614 24.6%
Low-engagement or dormant investors 551 22.0%
Pension builders approaching retirement 471 18.8%
High-value engaged investors 457 18.3%
Cash-heavy cautious investors 407 16.3%
2

What makes the segments meaningfully different?

Current interpretation

Highest cash-ratio segment: Cash-heavy cautious investors (52.7%).

Lowest digital-activity segment: Pension builders approaching retirement (27.4 active days over six months).

  • Bubble size reflects mean AUM.
  • Position reflects service needs, not suitability or risk advice.
3

Which communication route appears strongest by segment?

What this chart can support

  • Prioritising which education or reminder route to test further.
  • Comparing segment-level response rather than applying one message to everyone.
  • It cannot support a personal product recommendation.
4

How uncertain are the apparent best results?

How to read it

  • Dots are simulated uplift estimates.
  • Horizontal intervals show approximate 95% uncertainty.
  • Intervals crossing zero should be treated as directional rather than conclusive.
  • Each label also shows the combined treatment and control sample size.
Segment Strongest route Treatment n Control n Simulated uplift Evidence reading 95% interval
Cash-heavy cautious investors CASH TO INVEST EDUCATION 123 97 25.1 pp directional / uncertain 13.3 pp to 36.9 pp
Pension builders approaching retirement MANAGED SERVICE EDUCATION 112 112 14.3 pp positive simulated signal 4.6 pp to 23.9 pp
High-value engaged investors CASH TO INVEST EDUCATION 120 114 12.4 pp positive simulated signal 1.3 pp to 23.4 pp
Emerging monthly investors CASH TO INVEST EDUCATION 152 158 11.7 pp positive simulated signal 3.2 pp to 20.1 pp
Low-engagement or dormant investors ISA ALLOWANCE REMINDER 134 134 11.2 pp positive simulated signal 2.2 pp to 20.2 pp
5

Why not use the same route for every segment?

Interpretation

  • Rows show that segment response patterns differ.
  • Columns show that no route dominates in every group.
  • The heatmap is a prioritisation aid; the uncertainty view above is needed before making stronger claims.
6

Is the result based on a visible treatment-control comparison?

Why this matters

  • It separates baseline conversion from the treatment result.
  • The evidence table records treatment and control counts, conversions, rates, uplift, and intervals.
  • With real data, further checks would include randomisation integrity, pre-period balance, and multiple-testing control.
7

Do the segment names match measurable profiles?

Interpretation

  • Profile measures anchor the segment names in observed synthetic features.
  • This guards against labels that sound plausible but are not supported by the data.
  • A production version would also test stability across time, not only across random seeds.
Automated checks

Was the generated dataset and experiment output internally consistent?

These checks run from code and are saved to outputs/tables/data_quality_summary.csv.

Check Result Status
customer rows 2,500 PASS
unique customer ids 2,500 PASS
duplicate customer ids 0 PASS
missing customer cells 0 PASS
cash ratio out of range 0 PASS
negative aum rows 0 PASS
campaign rows 5,015 PASS
duplicate customer offer rows 0 PASS
treatment assignment share 51.2% PASS
minimum treatment or control group 91 REVIEW
segment offer cells 20 PASS
Boundary

What I would and would not claim

I would claim: the repository demonstrates a reproducible workflow connecting synthetic customer data, segmentation diagnostics, campaign comparisons, uncertainty, SQL checks, automated validation, and stakeholder reporting.

I would not claim: that the simulated uplift represents real customer behaviour, that k=5 is uniquely optimal, that the segments establish suitability, or that the dashboard gives regulated financial advice.

Production upgrades

What I would add with real data

Time-based segment stability, pre-treatment balance, multiple-testing adjustment, robust experimental design, dashboard freshness monitoring, privacy controls, and documented human approval before campaign use.
